title: Stabilize generated temp names across macro-only std changes
description: Snapshot updates currently churn Haxe-generated __hx suffixes when macro/std helper code changes, even when emitted Ruby semantics are unchanged. Investigate deterministic temp naming or snapshot normalization so unrelated macro additions do not ripple through runtime snapshots.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic per-scope Ruby temp local naming, regenerated snapshots to remove global TVar-id suffix churn, and added a snapshot guard against global-looking __hx suffixes. Verified with npm run test:core-subset, npm run test:active-record-model, npm run test:todoapp-rails, npm test.

title: Broaden RailsHx Turbo Stream action helpers
description: Extend the typed RailsHx server-side Turbo Streams facade beyond the initial append/replace/remove slice. Add before/after render and broadcast helpers where the Rails-native shape is known, and strengthen the smoke fixture so prepend/update/replace/remove broadcast shapes are asserted too.
acceptance_criteria: TurboStreams exposes before/after helpers for view/controller contexts and broadcastBeforeTo/broadcastAfterTo for server broadcasts; generated Ruby lowers to turbo_stream.before/after and Turbo::StreamsChannel.broadcast_before_to/broadcast_after_to; smoke coverage asserts prepend/update/before/after plus broadcast append/prepend/replace/update/remove/before/after; docs/compatibility mention the broader action set.
close_reason: Added before/after TurboStreams helpers, broadcastBeforeTo/broadcastAfterTo, stronger generated-Ruby assertions for the broader action family, and updated docs/compatibility.

title: Add typed ActiveRecord reorder helper
description: Add typed reorder(order:Order<TModel>) helpers for ActiveRecord models and relations. It should mirror order(), preserve Relation<TModel, TCriteria>, lower typed Order refs to Rails reorder(column: :direction), and reject orders from the wrong model through Haxe typing.
acceptance_criteria: Relation exposes reorder(order:Order<TModel>):Relation<TModel, TCriteria>; @:railsModel macro generates static reorder(order:Order<TModel>):Relation<TModel, TCriteria>; compiler lowers relation.reorder(Todo.f.id.desc()) and Todo.reorder(Todo.f.title.desc()) to Rails reorder(id: :desc)/reorder(title: :desc); active_record_model smoke asserts generated Ruby and invalid wrong-owner order usage fails; docs/README/compatibility/inventory mention typed reorder; snapshots are updated.
close_reason: Added typed model/relation reorder() with Order<TModel> validation, Rails reorder lowering, wrong-owner negative coverage, docs, and snapshots.

title: Author Haxe-owned Ruby modules and concerns
description: Add typed Haxe authoring for Ruby modules/ActiveSupport::Concern-style extension modules, so Haxe can emit reusable Ruby modules instead of relying on support Ruby files for @:rubyMixin implementations.
close_reason: Implemented Haxe-authored Ruby modules with @:rubyModule, initial @:rubyConcern output, typed include/extend contract inference, examples, docs, smoke tests, and snapshots.

title: Design typed ActiveStorage direct uploads and blob facades
description: Design RailsHx typed APIs for direct-upload seams, blob signed IDs, blob metadata/content-type/filename facades, and generated helper ergonomics. Preserve Rails-native ActiveStorage behavior while giving Haxe users typed direct-upload and blob contracts.
acceptance_criteria: Docs and examples show the supported direct-upload/blob path; compile-time types distinguish signed IDs, blobs, filenames, content types, and attachment refs where practical; generated Rails runtime or browser coverage validates at least one direct-upload/blob helper path; unsupported ActiveStorage-owned details are linked to follow-up beads with rationale.

title: Add typed ActiveRecord offset query helper
description: Add a small Rails-native ActiveRecord query ergonomics slice: typed offset(count:Int) on models and relations, preserving Relation<TModel, TCriteria> and lowering to normal ActiveRecord offset calls.
acceptance_criteria: Relation exposes offset(Int); @:railsModel macro generates static offset(Int) returning Relation<TModel, TCriteria>; active_record_model example and smoke assert Todo.offset and chained relation offset output; query docs and compatibility matrix mention offset; invalid offset value types fail through Haxe typing.
close_reason: Implemented typed ActiveRecord offset on relations and models with smoke, snapshot, docs, and invalid-type coverage.

title: Add typed Rails/Ruby library facades
description: Design and implement typed std/generator facades for common Rails/Ruby libraries and components, starting with ActiveSupport receiver extensions (for example blank?/present? style APIs) using @:rubyPatch contracts where appropriate. Use local references in ../haxe.compilerdev.reference/rails and ../haxe.compilerdev.reference/ruby, prefer macro/generated contracts when metadata can be inferred, and keep generated output Ruby/Rails-native.
close_reason: Added initial typed Rails/Ruby std facades for ActiveSupport receiver extensions: ObjectPresence and StringFilters use @:rubyPatch + Haxe using, compile to direct receiver calls, include docs/inventory, and have CI smoke coverage. Broader generator-assisted contract discovery remains tracked by haxe.ruby-y68.

title: Implement RailsHx typed ActiveRecord relation or
description: Add typed ActiveRecord Relation.or support so same-model relations compose with Rails-native or(...) output and wrong-model relations fail during Haxe compilation.
acceptance_criteria: Todo.where(...).or(Todo.where(...)) type-checks and emits normal Rails .or(...); relation chains remain typed after or; User relation operands are rejected by Haxe; active_record_model smoke/docs/snapshots/package checks are updated.
close_reason: Implemented typed ActiveRecord Relation.or with @:native("or") output, added same-model relation composition coverage, mixed-model negative compile coverage, docs, compatibility/roadmap/inventory updates, and snapshots.

title: Implement RailsHx named multi-field Projection.pluck
description: Implement the chosen named-object multi-field projection API from docs/railshx-projections-grouping-design.md. Projection.pluck(source, {key: Model.f.field, ...}) should return Array<{...}> with field-value types and lower through ActiveRecord pluck(:field, ...).
acceptance_criteria: Projection.pluck accepts model class or Relation source; projection spec must be a non-empty object literal of same-owner generated field refs; generated Ruby includes ActiveRecord pluck(:id, :title) and runtime row shaping; active_record_model has positive and wrong-owner/empty-spec negative coverage; query docs, inventory, package checks, and snapshots are updated.
close_reason: Implemented Projection.pluck as a typed macro facade for model/relation sources, lowered it to Rails pluck plus HXRuby row shaping, added positive and negative ActiveRecord smoke coverage, docs, inventory, gap report, and snapshots.

title: Harden ActionMailer attachments and mailer-job integration

acceptance_criteria: Typed attachment builders cover common hash/inline/content-type paths or are explicitly deferred; runtime Rails tests assert at least one non-string attachment case; docs show ActionMailer + ActiveJob delivery workflow; unchecked attachment interop remains named and documented.

title: Add RailsHx typed includes joins and scopes

close_reason: Added typed ActiveRecord association refs plus includes/joins lowering and wrong-owner negative coverage.

title: Implement RailsHx typed multi-field order
description: Add typed multi-field ActiveRecord order/reorder support via generated Order refs, preserving owner checks and Rails-shaped output.
acceptance_criteria: Order.many([Todo.f.title.asc(), Todo.f.id.desc()]) compiles as Order<Todo>; Todo.order(...) and relation.reorder(...) lower to Rails order(title: :asc, id: :desc)/reorder(...); wrong-owner mixed orders fail during Haxe compilation; active_record_model smoke, query docs, README, roadmap/compatibility, snapshots, and package/inventory checks are updated.
close_reason: Implemented typed multi-field ActiveRecord ordering via Order.many, added model-level order stubs, lowered arrays of typed order refs to Rails-native order/reorder keyword args, covered mixed-model failures, updated ActiveRecord smoke, docs, compatibility/roadmap/inventory, and snapshots.

title: Add typed ActiveRecord last query helper

close_reason: Added typed model/relation last() helpers with ActiveRecord smoke assertions, docs, inventory, compatibility, and snapshot coverage.

notes: Working this as the immediate follow-up to Expr/Predicate. Scope: keep public whereGt/whereGte/whereLt/whereLte and negated variants unchanged, but route compiler lowering through the same expression predicate helper used by whereExpr(...). Add/keep smoke coverage proving generated Ruby is unchanged and update docs/notes so future predicates extend one path.
close_reason: Unified existing whereGt/whereGte/whereLt/whereLte and negated convenience predicates with the Expr/Predicate compiler backend by routing both authoring surfaces through activeRecordExpressionPredicateArg. Generated Ruby shape remains unchanged; ActiveRecord smoke verifies existing comparison output and Expr predicate output. Docs now state the shared backend and recommended usage split.

title: Audit std helpers that can lower directly to idiomatic Ruby
description: Several std/Math and std/runtime helpers may delegate to HXRuby even when Ruby already has an equivalent receiver/module method, e.g. Math.abs -> value.abs. Audit similar cases and inline/direct-lower them when Ruby semantics match Haxe. Keep HXRuby helpers only where they encode Haxe-specific behavior, coercion/NaN/-0.0 edge cases, cross-version Ruby compatibility, or runtime reuse that materially improves correctness.
close_reason: Audited std Math helpers and direct-lowered Math.abs to value.abs and Math.isFinite to value.finite? because Ruby receiver semantics match Haxe for typed Float values. Kept HXRuby helpers where they still encode Haxe behavior, including min/max NaN propagation, domain-error-to-NaN math, pow, round, and isNaN for Integer-safe checks. Removed unused math_abs/math_finite? runtime helpers and added stdlib smoke assertions for the direct Ruby shapes. Verified with npm run test:runtime-minitest, npm run test:stdlib-mvp, UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1 npm run test:snapshots, npm test.

title: Add typed ActiveRecord distinct query helper
description: Add typed distinct() helpers for ActiveRecord models and relations. The helper should return Relation<TModel, TCriteria>, lower to normal Rails distinct calls, and preserve typed relation composition.
acceptance_criteria: Relation exposes distinct():Relation<TModel, TCriteria>; @:railsModel macro generates static distinct():Relation<TModel, TCriteria>; active_record_model example and smoke assert Todo.distinct() and relation.distinct() generated Ruby; query docs, README, compatibility matrix, and inventory mention distinct(); snapshots are updated.
close_reason: Added typed model/relation distinct() helpers with ActiveRecord smoke assertions, docs, roadmap/inventory/compatibility updates, and snapshot coverage.

title: Implement std Array support
description: Add Ruby-backed Haxe Array API support with portable semantics where Ruby methods differ; update std inventory/gap reports and add smoke/snapshot coverage.
close_reason: Implemented Ruby-backed Array API lowering/runtime helpers for Haxe Array semantics, added stdlib smoke coverage, fixed increment/decrement statement lowering exposed by array comprehensions, refreshed snapshots, and updated std inventory/gap reports.

title: Implement RailsHx Expr and Predicate core
description: Add rails.active_record.Expr<TModel,TValue> and Predicate<TModel> as typed Haxe-only tokens. Support Expr.field(...), Expr.lower(...), comparison predicate methods, Relation.whereExpr(...), Relation.whereNotExpr(...), and compiler lowering to Rails/Arel-backed Ruby. Keep casual strings fail-closed: no from String conversions, owner/value-type mismatches fail at Haxe compile time, and examples include negative compile tests.
notes: Implementing first concrete RailsHx typed expression slice from the SQL/string policy: Expr<TModel,TValue>, Predicate<TModel>, Relation.whereExpr/whereNotExpr, and typed lower(...) ordering. This bead is intentionally narrow; richer functions, aggregate aliases, having, and explicit Sql.* escapes are tracked separately.
close_reason: Implemented RailsHx Expr<TModel,TValue> and Predicate<TModel> tokens, model/relation whereExpr/whereNotExpr stubs, compiler lowering to Rails/Arel predicates, positive ActiveRecord examples, negative compile coverage for raw strings/wrong owners/wrong value types/non-predicate shapes, docs, inventory, and snapshots.

title: Design typed ActionCable connection auth and identifier APIs
description: Design typed RailsHx support for ActionCable connection classes, identified_by declarations, current-user/session/cookie auth seams, and reject_unauthorized_connection. Preserve Rails-native ActionCable output and expose typed connection identifiers to channels where practical.
acceptance_criteria: Docs and examples show the supported typed connection/auth path; generated Ruby remains normal ActionCable::Connection::Base/Channel::Base shape; Rails connection or channel tests cover accepted and rejected connections; unsupported auth patterns are explicitly documented with escape hatches or follow-up beads.

title: Implement RailsHx typed numeric ActiveRecord aggregations
description: Add typed numeric aggregation helpers for RailsHx ActiveRecord queries. Start with Rails-shaped sum/average over generated model field refs, preserve model/relation source typing, reject wrong-owner fields, and document generated Rails Ruby.
acceptance_criteria: Todo.sum(Todo.f.userId) and relation.sum(Todo.f.userId) compile and lower to sum(:user_id); average over supported numeric fields compiles with a nullable numeric return; wrong-owner aggregation fields fail during Haxe compilation; active_record_model smoke, query docs, example README, roadmap/compatibility, snapshots, inventory/package checks are updated.
close_reason: Implemented v1 typed ActiveRecord sum/average for Int field refs on models and relations, lowered them to Rails sum(:field)/average(:field), added positive and negative ActiveRecord smoke coverage, docs, inventory, roadmap/compatibility, and snapshots.

title: RailsHx production readiness
description: Track the remaining work required for haxe.ruby/RailsHx to be credible for production Rails applications, beyond compiler/snapshot proof-of-concepts.
acceptance_criteria: Production readiness is documented with explicit gates; child beads cover mandatory Rails runtime CI, deployable dogfood evidence, typed API completeness, escape-hatch/security policy, generator/dev/prod UX, gradual adoption/interop, docs/support, and release criteria; README/roadmap/compatibility docs link to the readiness contract.

title: Make Rails runtime CI mandatory and broad
description: Harden Rails runtime confidence so production readiness does not rely on optional local skips.
acceptance_criteria: CI runs npm run test:rails-runtime as a required gate on supported Ruby/Rails lanes; missing Rails gems are hard failures in CI; runtime failures identify compiler/materialization/migration/request/browser stage; docs explain local vs CI behavior.
close_reason: Rails runtime CI is mandatory across the supported Ruby matrix, release contracts guard the lane, and runtime/browser smoke logs identify failing stages.

title: Integrate RailsHx ActiveRecord SQL/string escape policy
description: Follow-up from haxe.ruby-bjv.3.1. Apply the approved SQL/string-bearing API policy to ActiveRecord query APIs that cannot be fully represented by field refs, such as raw where/order/select/group/having fragments and Arel-like expressions.
acceptance_criteria: ActiveRecord raw SQL/string APIs are either replaced by typed builders or explicit audited escape hatches; strict-boundary tests reject casual strings/raw SQL in production examples; docs explain safe usage and generated Ruby shape.
notes: RailsHx SQL/string policy hardening now includes typed Aggregate expression builders, Group.countHaving aggregate having predicates, and Projection.group selected grouped aggregate result rows. Raw having/projection strings remain rejected in examples/tests; remaining query SQL policy work is richer grouped keys, joined aggregate projections, broader SQL function builders, and any explicit Sql.* escapes for gaps.

title: R&D: investigate RailsHx to Phoenix/Elixir migration adapter path
description: Research whether RailsHx/Haxe can support an optional compile-time adapter/translation layer that lets parts of a RailsHx app, or eventually a whole RailsHx app, target Phoenix/Elixir. The goal is not immediate implementation: investigate whether Haxe can preserve idiomatic Rails authoring while enabling gradual migration to Phoenix/Elixir, mixed Ruby/Elixir components, or parallel idiomatic Haxe.Elixir authoring through adapters/translations. This should compare compile-level translation, generated boundary adapters, runtime interop, framework semantic gaps, data/schema/query portability, templates/components, controllers/routes, jobs/mailers/realtime, and gradual migration workflows.
acceptance_criteria: Before implementation starts, the user must run a GPT 5.5 Pro research pass and return insights plus proposed bead/plan mutations. The R&D output should document feasibility, recommended architecture options, risks, non-goals, required collaboration with haxe.elixir.codex/PhoenixHx, and concrete follow-up beads if the idea is worth pursuing.
notes: Low-priority end-of-graph R&D only. Do not start coding from this bead directly. First ask the user to consult GPT 5.5 Pro and bring back the analysis/plan mutations; use those results to split implementation/design beads. Any viable approach must work with and coordinate against ../haxe.elixir.codex/PhoenixHx rather than inventing an incompatible parallel Elixir target path.

title: R&D: plan typed and optimized Ruby stdlib coverage
description: Research how haxe.ruby should track, manage, prioritize, implement, and support the whole Ruby standard library as a Haxe-typed and Ruby-optimized authoring layer. This is not immediate implementation; it should design the inventory, coverage tiers, typed extern/facade strategy, macro/generator opportunities, runtime-helper policy, optimization policy, conformance testing, docs, and long-term maintenance model for Ruby stdlib support.
acceptance_criteria: Produce an R&D report that inventories Ruby stdlib domains and prioritizes coverage tiers; defines which APIs should be typed externs, Haxe-owned facades, macros, abstracts, or optimized compiler lowering; identifies where Ruby-native direct calls should replace HXRuby runtime helpers; proposes tests using local Ruby docs/spec references and generated smoke fixtures; defines compatibility/version policy across supported Ruby versions; and splits concrete implementation beads for the first useful stdlib slices.
notes: Low-priority R&D. Use ../haxe.compilerdev.reference/ruby and local Ruby docs/source/spec references where useful. Compare current Haxe std/runtime helpers against Ruby-native APIs and identify opportunities for direct optimized lowering without breaking portable semantics. Do not start broad stdlib implementation until this report produces a staged bead plan.

title: Add deployable RailsHx dogfood production app
description: Create or promote a RailsHx app that exercises production build/deploy concerns, not only smoke fixtures.
acceptance_criteria: A deployable app compiles Haxe/HHX/client JS, runs migrations, passes rails test, passes zeitwerk:check and assets:precompile, documents production build steps, and has a CI or scripted smoke proving the release artifact contains generated files.
close_reason: Added a CI-backed RailsHx production dogfood lane that compiles server/client Haxe, runs Rails migrations/tests, passes Zeitwerk/assets precompile, and validates a release archive with generated artifacts.

title: Audit and close RailsHx typed API production gaps
description: Inventory Rails/RailsHx API gaps that block realistic production apps and convert them into typed implementation beads.
acceptance_criteria: Gap audit covers ActiveRecord advanced queries/scopes/transactions/validations/migrations/controllers/templates/jobs/mailers/Turbo/ActionCable/ActiveStorage; each P0/P1 production blocker has a bead; raw string/SQL-bearing APIs have explicit typed or escape-hatch policy before implementation.
close_reason: Documented RailsHx typed API production gap audit, linked it from readiness/compatibility docs, and created P1 implementation beads for each production-blocking API surface plus SQL/string policy.

title: Close RailsHx ActiveRecord query/scope/transaction production gaps
description: Implement the production ActiveRecord query surface beyond the current relation slice: typed scopes, transactions, locking, association-aware criteria, nested joins/includes, raw SQL policy, and safe escape hatches.
acceptance_criteria: Typed scope builders compose like Rails scopes; transactions and locking have Rails-shaped typed APIs; association-aware criteria and nested joins/includes are covered; string/SQL-bearing APIs follow the approved typed-or-explicit escape-hatch policy; active_record_model and Rails runtime/dogfood tests cover representative production queries.
notes: Implemented typed transaction and pessimistic locking slice: Todo.transaction(block, options), TransactionOptions/TransactionIsolation, relation/model lock(?Lock), compiler lowering to Rails transaction/lock calls, active_record_model docs/smoke/snapshots. Added relation-level find hardening: Relation.find now uses Int/String overloads instead of Dynamic while static model find remains primary-key typed. Remaining production gaps are tracked by sibling epic beads haxe.ruby-bjv.8 (nested association criteria/includes), haxe.ruby-bjv.9 (scope DSL), and haxe.ruby-bjv.10 (SQL/string policy integration).

title: Close RailsHx model, validation, association, and migration production gaps
description: Harden model metadata and migration APIs for production Rails apps: validations, callbacks, associations, enums, schema evolution, indexes, constraints, reversible operations, and schema-history behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Common Rails validations/callbacks/association options are typed and documented; migration operations cover production schema changes including constraints/indexes/foreign keys/reversible paths; destructive or raw migration escapes are explicit; generated migrations pass Rails runtime and production dogfood checks.

title: Close RailsHx controller, routing, and request lifecycle production gaps
description: Harden typed ActionController and routing coverage for real apps: before/after/around filters, redirects, respond_to, request variants, rescue handling, auth-friendly hooks, route/controller validation, and request specs.
acceptance_criteria: Controller APIs remain Rails-shaped and typed; route helpers and render/redirect/head/respond_to surfaces reject wrong arguments; request lifecycle hooks are method-backed where possible; errors/rescue and auth-oriented extension points are documented; Rails runtime request tests cover representative flows.
notes: Controller response typing slice: added RenderOptions and RedirectOptions for direct ActionController render/redirect helpers, split redirectTo(String) from redirectToOptions(...), made Status explicit-only so raw status strings are rejected, and added negative compile coverage for invalid render/redirect statuses. Added initial typed respond_to slice: Base.respondTo(...) exposes the Rails format collector as rails.action_controller.Responder with block-checked html/json/turboStream/xml/any methods; action-controller smoke verifies generated respond_to Ruby and rejects invalid responder callback arguments. Added typed request format slice: request().format() now returns RequestFormat with typed html/json/turboStream/xml/any predicates and toString/symbol access; action-controller smoke verifies Rails-native request.format.json?/to_s output and rejects assigning the format object to String. Added contextual controller lifecycle DSL: @:railsController classes must declare static final lifecycle = { ... } (or [] for no lifecycle declarations), ControllerDsl beforeAction/afterAction/aroundAction/rescueFrom validate method/action/exception refs, generated Ruby emits Rails before_action/after_action/around_action/rescue_from class macros, rescueFromNamed is the checked Ruby-constant interop escape, and action-controller smoke covers generated output plus missing/malformed lifecycle failures. Remaining controller production work includes richer content negotiation, variants, auth hooks, streaming/send_file, CSRF policy, route/controller validation, and request/runtime specs.

title: Close RailsHx HHX, ActionView, forms, and component production gaps
description: Broaden typed HHX/ActionView coverage for production Rails templates: form builders, collection rendering, layouts, content_for/yield, helpers, partials/components, errors, and interop with Rails-owned ERB.
acceptance_criteria: Canonical templates use HHX by default; common Rails form/helper/component patterns have typed APIs; collection rendering and locals are checked; content_for/layout/slots are covered; Rails-owned ERB interop remains explicit and checked; todoapp/dogfood and negative compile tests cover the new helper surface.
notes: ActionView hardening progressed: Component slot names validate at macro time, checked Template.existing/Component.existing missing files fail closed, and controller layout rendering now uses typed Layout tokens via Template.layout(ViewClass) or checked Layout.named(...). Remaining: broader ActionView helper breadth, form/component ergonomics, collection rendering, accessibility helpers, and runtime Rails coverage.

title: Close RailsHx ActionMailer, ActiveJob, and ActiveStorage production gaps
description: Move background jobs, mailers, and storage from initial typed slices to production-usable RailsHx surfaces with runtime coverage.
acceptance_criteria: Mailer previews/tests, multipart templates, parameterized mailers, job adapters/test helpers, retry/discard semantics, attachment validations, direct-upload/storage helpers, and runtime Rails tests are covered or explicitly deferred with beads; APIs remain Rails-native and typed at Haxe boundaries.
notes: Closed production ActiveJob slice haxe.ruby-71o: generated Rails runtime lane now asserts typed argument serialization/deserialization and retry_on re-enqueue behavior in addition to queue/enqueue/perform. Adapter-specific discard/failure diagnostics remain tracked by follow-up ActiveJob adapter/discard bead.
close_reason: Closed production-gap umbrella by adding a generated Rails ActiveJob runtime lane with ActiveJob::TestHelper queue/enqueue/perform assertions under REQUIRE_RAILS=1, wiring it into npm run test:rails-runtime, documenting the runtime strategy, and filing explicit follow-up beads for remaining ActiveJob adapter/serialization/retry-discard work (haxe.ruby-71o), ActionMailer previews/parameterized/delivery tests (haxe.ruby-if3), and ActiveStorage runtime/direct-upload/storage helpers (haxe.ruby-cfj).

title: Close RailsHx Turbo, Turbo Streams, and ActionCable production gaps
description: Harden realtime/progressive RailsHx APIs: typed Turbo client helpers, server-side streams, stream targets/names, ActionCable channel params/payloads, subscriptions, and browser/runtime tests.
acceptance_criteria: Turbo frame/stream/client APIs cover common Rails/Hotwire flows without behavior-bearing string drift; ActionCable channels and JS subscriptions have typed params/payloads; Playwright and Rails runtime/channel tests prove representative flows; unsupported raw stream/channel names are explicit escape hatches.
notes: 2026-06-15: Added generated Rails ActionCable runtime lane to npm run test:action-cable. The lane materializes a Rails 7.2.3.1 app, uses ActionCable::Channel::TestCase against the generated channel, and asserts subscription confirmation, typed stream subscription, perform :ping transmissions, and broadcast payloads. It is wired into npm run test:rails-runtime via REQUIRE_RAILS=1. Local non-runtime mode still skips when Rails bundle is unavailable and mandatory mode has Ruby-version preflight. Remaining realtime production gaps are tracked explicitly as haxe.ruby-tke (ActionCable connection identifiers/reject/confirm/stream lifecycle) and haxe.ruby-7bx (broader Turbo frame/stream browser coverage).
close_reason: Closed realtime production-gap umbrella by adding generated Rails ActionCable::Channel::TestCase runtime coverage for subscribe/stream/perform/broadcast, keeping typed Turbo/ActionCable compile/browser gates intact, wiring ActionCable into npm run test:rails-runtime, and filing explicit follow-up beads for deeper ActionCable lifecycle/auth and broader Turbo frame/stream browser coverage.

title: Design RailsHx typed policy for SQL and string-bearing APIs
description: Define when RailsHx APIs may accept strings/symbols/SQL fragments and when they must require typed refs/builders. This covers ActiveRecord where/order/select/group/having/from/joins, migrations, templates, routes, Turbo targets, and external Rails paths.
acceptance_criteria: A documented policy classifies each string/SQL-bearing surface as typed default, checked literal, or explicit escape hatch; escape hatches are named and auditable; implementation beads for affected APIs reference the policy; strict-boundary tests reject accidental raw SQL/string drift in canonical examples.
close_reason: Documented RailsHx SQL/string-bearing policy with typed default, checked literal, and explicit escape-hatch classes; linked affected implementation beads; added npm run test:sql-string-policy to reject raw query strings in canonical examples.

title: Audit production escape hatches and security policy
description: Make unsafe boundaries explicit before production use: raw Ruby, raw ERB, raw SQL/string clauses, Dynamic, unchecked template paths, external Ruby contracts.
acceptance_criteria: Strict-mode docs and tests cover raw __ruby__, raw ERB, SQL-ish strings, template/path filesystem checks, Dynamic boundaries, and generator inference; canonical samples avoid unsafe escapes; every allowed escape hatch is named, documented, opt-in, and has follow-up beads for typed replacements where appropriate.
close_reason: Documented RailsHx escape-hatch/security audit, linked it from production docs and AGENTS, verified existing strict/path/raw-ERB/adoption gates, and created P1 follow-up beads for SQL/string, Dynamic, path/template, and generator/LLM hardening.

title: Harden RailsHx SQL/string escape hatch enforcement
description: Turn the SQL/string-bearing policy into enforcement for RailsHx APIs that can carry SQL or behavior-bearing strings.
acceptance_criteria: ActiveRecord/migration/template/Turbo APIs classify strings as typed default, checked literal, or explicit escape; raw SQL fragments use named auditable APIs; strict-boundary tests reject accidental SQL/string drift in canonical samples; docs link each allowed escape to a safer typed alternative or follow-up bead.
notes: Initial enforcement slice complete via haxe.ruby-k25. Explicit SQL escape hatch API slice complete via haxe.ruby-elx. Turbo string-hardening complete for StreamTarget/StreamName. ActionCable string-hardening complete for Stream<TPayload>/SubscriptionParam<TValue>. Template/path hardening includes checked existing-template/component files, macro-validated component slot names, and typed Layout tokens rejecting raw layout strings. Controller lifecycle rescue constants now default to typed exception refs via rescueFrom(Type, handler); rescueFromNamed("Ruby::Constant", handler) is documented as a safe Ruby constant path escape and still validates the handler. Remaining work: broader migration string classifications and follow-up SQL surfaces such as select/group/having once those APIs are expanded.
close_reason: Completed SQL/string escape-hatch enforcement pass: ActiveRecord raw SQL routes through typed Sql.unsafeWhere/unsafeOrder relation APIs, canonical examples reject casual raw query strings, template/path/Turbo/ActionCable/controller string surfaces are classified and guarded, rescueFromNamed remains checked by safe Ruby constant paths, and migrations now validate externalTables as safe Rails table identifiers while preserving typed knownModels/operation checks. Remaining richer select/group/having/aggregate design work stays tracked by haxe.ruby-bjv.10, haxe.ruby-deg, and haxe.ruby-6ri.

title: Harden RailsHx Dynamic boundary policy and replacements
description: Audit app-facing RailsHx Dynamic usage and replace ordinary production paths with typed facades, abstracts, or generated contracts.
acceptance_criteria: Dynamic usage is classified as runtime-required, compiler/internal, interop escape, or replacement target; canonical samples avoid app-level Dynamic except documented JS/Rails runtime boundaries; route params, Turbo details, ActionCable subscriptions, ActiveSupport subscriptions, and generator-produced externs have typed replacements or child beads.
notes: Implemented typed Rails route helper params with rails.routing.RouteParam. Added relation-level ActiveRecord find Dynamic hardening: Relation.find now uses Int/String overloads instead of Dynamic, rejects object-shaped IDs in active_record_model negative compile coverage, and emits direct Rails find(...) calls without helper runtime. Added Turbo client boundary hardening: TurboTypedEvent/TurboSubmitEvent expose EventTarget targets, Turbo.addFetchRequestHeader(...) wraps runtime-owned fetchOptions mutation, todoapp client no longer uses app-level Dynamic for submit targets/header mutation/storage catches, and turbo/todoapp smokes cover the helper. Added ActionCable client consumer hardening: Consumer.create() returns a nominal Consumer handle, Consumer.subscribe/TodosCableClient.subscribe require Consumer instead of Dynamic, raw object consumers fail in action-cable smoke, and docs/audit/inventory record the boundary. Added ActionCable perform hardening: Subscription is now an opaque JS handle facade, perform(...) requires typed Action<TData> tokens and matching payloads, performUnchecked(...) is the explicit legacy interop escape, and action-cable smoke rejects raw action strings/wrong payloads. Added ActionMailer options/attachments hardening: MailAddress and MailLayout replace Dynamic recipient/layout fields, MailRenderOptions types render kwargs, Attachments.add accepts typed string content, unchecked mailer escapes are explicit, action-mailer smoke rejects raw recipient objects/non-string attachments, and compiler keyword cleanup keeps abstract-backed kwargs Rails-shaped. Added ActiveSupport subscription handle hardening: Subscription is now opaque without implicit from Dynamic, unsubscribe({}) fails in instrumentation smoke, and docs/audit/inventory document the handle boundary. Added ActiveStorage attachable hardening: One.attach accepts String, Many.attach accepts Array<String>, raw Rails attachable payloads require explicit attachUnchecked(...), invalid object-shaped default attach fails in active-storage smoke, and docs/audit/inventory document the escape. Added controller Dynamic boundary hardening: direct ActionController render/redirect helpers now use RenderOptions/RedirectOptions with Status-typed status kwargs; redirectTo(String) remains the Rails path/location helper; redirectToOptions(...) handles Rails option-hash redirects; raw string statuses fail in action-controller negative compile coverage. Added request format Dynamic hardening: request().format() now returns typed RequestFormat instead of Dynamic, common MIME predicates are typed, and invalid assignment to String fails in action-controller negative compile coverage. Remaining Dynamic work includes generator-inferred review placeholders, broad Rails-owned render payloads such as json/locals/layout, and deeper controller lifecycle surfaces tracked by production gap beads.
close_reason: Completed Dynamic boundary hardening pass: app-facing Dynamic uses are classified in the escape-hatch audit, canonical RailsHx APIs use typed facades/abstracts/generated contracts, and remaining runtime-owned/generator/controller surfaces are documented under production follow-up beads. This slice also removes ActiveJob helper Dynamic returns: performNow preserves perform return type, performLater returns rails.active_job.Base, and active-job smoke now proves valid and invalid return typing.

title: Harden RailsHx template/path escape hatch enforcement
description: Make all RailsHx template/path escape hatches auditable and fail-closed by default.
acceptance_criteria: Template.of/layout/existing remain the default checked paths; Template.named/external and @:railsTemplate string paths are literal-only, safe-shape checked, and documented as lower-level escapes; missing external files fail closed where discovery is possible; unchecked escapes require comments/docs and negative tests; canonical samples avoid unchecked paths.
notes: Template/path hardening progressed: Component.of/existing validate slot names at macro time, Component.existing and Template.existing have missing-file negative coverage, and renderTemplateWithLayout now requires a typed Layout token. Template.layout(ViewClass) remains the default class-backed layout path; lower-level literals must use checked Layout.named(...); raw layout strings are rejected in todoapp smoke. Remaining: broader unchecked Template.named/external policy docs/tests and any additional generated-template path surfaces.
close_reason: Completed template/path escape hardening: Template.of/layout/existing remain the checked defaults; Template.named/external and @:railsTemplate paths are literal-only and safe-shape checked; Template.existing/Component.existing fail closed against app/views or rails/app/views; raw layout strings and unsafe paths have negative coverage; unchecked Template.external remains documented as a lower-level escape. This slice also rejects raw backslash paths across Template, Component, ViewMacro, MailerMacro, and HHX compiler lowering, with todoapp smoke regression coverage.

title: Harden RailsHx generator and LLM-assisted inference security
description: Lock down generator-assisted Rails adoption so inferred contracts are deterministic, reviewable, and fail-closed.
acceptance_criteria: RBS/YARD/Ripper/Rails metadata inputs fail closed when missing or unsafe; discovery modes remain suggest-only unless explicitly writing checked wrappers; LLM assistance is documented as advisory patches only; generated Dynamic placeholders are marked for review; tests cover missing files, overwrite protection, and no execution of app code during inference.
close_reason: Completed generator/LLM-assisted inference hardening: adoption inputs that emit Haxe source now validate package names, Ruby constants, template paths, local names, and Haxe type strings; source/RBS/extension files must stay inside the app/output root and fail closed when missing; source-backed inference remains Ripper/RBS parsing only and does not execute app code; existing wrappers remain overwrite-protected; smoke coverage asserts unsafe inputs, missing files, overwrite protection, Dynamic review comments, and parser-only behavior. LLM assistance remains documented as advisory only.

title: Harden RailsHx generator and dev/prod UX
description: Ensure creating, adopting, developing, watching, testing, and producing RailsHx apps feels better than vanilla Rails plus manual Haxe ceremony.
acceptance_criteria: Install/scaffold/adopt/route generators are Ruby-native and tested; generated apps include clear bin/rake/dev/prod workflows; watch/client watch integrate with Rails conventions; errors are actionable; production compile/precompile flow is documented and smoke-tested.
close_reason: Added RailsHx production rake/bin workflow, generator/package/release smoke coverage, and updated dev/prod docs.

title: Harden gradual adoption and mixed Rails interop for production
description: Make RailsHx safe for incrementally adopting existing Ruby/ERB/Rails apps and for Ruby code consuming Haxe output.
acceptance_criteria: Examples cover Ruby consuming Haxe services/templates, Haxe consuming existing Ruby/ERB/components, RBS/source-backed contract generation, missing-file fail-closed behavior, and runtime checks for interop boundaries; docs explain recommended migration paths.

title: Publish RailsHx production readiness checklist and support contract
description: Document what production-ready means, current status, required gates, and how users should evaluate risk before adopting RailsHx.
acceptance_criteria: Docs state current maturity, production readiness criteria, mandatory commands, known blockers, supported Ruby/Rails/Haxe/Node versions, release/support expectations, and links to all production-readiness beads.
close_reason: Published the RailsHx production readiness checklist/support contract in docs/railshx-production-readiness.md, linked it from README/roadmap/compatibility, and added AGENTS guidance requiring production blockers to be tracked under haxe.ruby-bjv.

title: Add RailsHx nested association criteria and nested joins/includes
description: Follow-up from haxe.ruby-bjv.3.1. Extend typed ActiveRecord criteria and association refs beyond flat owner fields so RailsHx can express nested association criteria and nested includes/joins/preload/eager_load shapes without raw strings.
acceptance_criteria: Typed nested association criteria and nested association loading APIs are designed, implemented, documented, and covered by active_record_model compile/runtime-shape tests; wrong association owners and invalid nested fields fail during Haxe compilation.
notes: Implemented typed nested association loading slice: Association.nested(parent, child) with Haxe owner/target chain validation; model/relation preload and eagerLoad APIs; compiler lowering to Rails includes({user: :todos}), preload({user: :todos}), and eager_load({user: :todos}); active_record_model positive/negative smoke coverage and docs. Remaining under this bead: nested association criteria design/implementation for where/findBy/exists-style filters.
close_reason: Implemented typed nested association criteria for where/findBy/exists with nested Rails hash lowering, active_record_model docs/examples, snapshot coverage, and invalid nested field/type compile-failure smoke tests.

title: Add RailsHx production scope declaration DSL
description: Follow-up from haxe.ruby-bjv.3.1. Add a Rails-shaped typed scope declaration layer beyond ad-hoc static methods, including lambda-like scope builders, optional default_scope decisions, and generated Ruby scope shape.
acceptance_criteria: Typed scope declarations compose as ActiveRecord relations, preserve IntelliSense and return types, emit recognizable Rails scope/default_scope code where approved, and have positive/negative active_record_model tests.
close_reason: Implemented typed Rails scope declarations with @:railsScope and @:railsDefaultScope, Rails scope/default_scope artifact emission, active_record_model positive and negative coverage, snapshots, and docs.

title: Design RailsHx multi-field projections and grouped counts
description: Lock the v1 API and implementation plan for named-object multi-field projections and typed grouped counts before compiler implementation. The design should choose public APIs, generated Ruby shapes, invalid cases, tests, and follow-up implementation beads.
acceptance_criteria: Design doc exists with chosen Haxe APIs, inferred result types, generated Rails Ruby shapes, invalid/wrong-owner examples, implementation notes, out-of-scope boundaries, and follow-up implementation beads for projections and grouped counts.
close_reason: Added the RailsHx multi-field projection and grouped-count design, linked it from query docs/roadmap, and created concrete implementation follow-up beads with dependencies.

title: Audit RailsHx smoke-only surfaces for snapshot-first coverage
description: Review RailsHx surfaces whose main generated-output contract is currently guarded mostly by smoke/runtime tests, then add or extend committed snapshots for canonical generated Ruby/ERB/JS shape. Keep smoke tests focused on negative typing, syntax, and Rails consumption seams rather than duplicating snapshot output checks.
acceptance_criteria: A testing audit lists RailsHx surfaces with snapshot coverage and gaps; ActionCable/Turbo/generator surfaces have explicit snapshot coverage or linked follow-up rationale; docs state snapshot-first policy and smoke/runtime supporting roles; npm run test:snapshots remains green.
notes: Snapshot-first smoke audit progressed: components, Turbo Streams, ActiveSupport instrumentation, and Rails engine/plugin output now have committed snapshot coverage. Their smoke scripts were reduced to justified non-snapshot checks: required files where useful, Ruby syntax, negative compile/type-safety diagnostics, optional runtime consumption seams, unsafe path rejection, and generator CLI behavior. Remaining smoke-heavy surface: Rails interop/adoption.
close_reason: Completed snapshot-first audit: ActionCable, components, Turbo Streams, ActiveSupport instrumentation, Rails engine/plugin, and Rails interop/adoption now have committed generated-output snapshots. Corresponding smoke scripts were trimmed to non-snapshot value: syntax, negative typing/fail-closed checks, runtime consumption seams, unsafe-path checks, materialization, and generator/package behavior. Testing strategy documents snapshot-first policy and justified smoke/check-only surfaces.

title: Implement RailsHx typed ActiveRecord reverseOrder
description: Add typed ActiveRecord reverseOrder support on models and relations, preserving Haxe casing while emitting Rails-native reverse_order().
acceptance_criteria: Todo.reverseOrder() and relation.reverseOrder() type-check as Relation<Todo, criteria>, emit reverse_order() calls, preserve chaining after reverseOrder, and active_record_model smoke/docs/snapshots/package checks are updated.
close_reason: Implemented typed ActiveRecord reverseOrder on generated model stubs and Relation, preserving Haxe camelCase while emitting Rails-native reverse_order(), with ActiveRecord smoke, docs, inventory/roadmap/compatibility, and snapshot updates.

title: Harden RailsHx ActiveStorage runtime storage helpers and direct-upload seams
description: Move ActiveStorage from typed attachment refs/static lowering toward production runtime confidence. Cover attachment validations, blob/service assumptions, variants/previews, purge/analyze helpers, direct-upload seams, and typed builders for common attachable hash shapes.
acceptance_criteria: Generated Rails runtime tests prove attach/read/purge for one and many attachments under REQUIRE_RAILS=1; direct-upload and variant/blob helper gaps are implemented or explicitly deferred; unchecked attachable shapes have typed replacements or documented escape-hatch beads; docs state supported storage assumptions.
close_reason: Added generated Rails ActiveStorage runtime tests for one/many attachment signed-ID attach, download/read, and purge using ActiveRecord, ActiveStorage tables, SQLite, and test disk storage; wired test:active-storage into test:rails-runtime and release contracts; documented supported storage assumptions and deferred direct-upload/blob/variant/validation/builder work to haxe.ruby-33p and haxe.ruby-9sl.

title: Adopt ruby_first profile naming
description: Make ruby_first the preferred public name for the Ruby-first profile while preserving idiomatic as a compatibility alias; update profile docs and agent guidance.
close_reason: Added ruby_first as the preferred Ruby profile spelling, kept idiomatic compatibility aliases, documented one-pipeline profile semantics, added profile resolver CI coverage, and updated snapshots after deterministic temp-id churn.

title: Design typed selected aggregate result objects for RailsHx
description: Follow-up to haxe.ruby-6ri. Design and implement selected aggregate aliases/result row shapes on top of RailsHx projection/expression layers, e.g. count/sum/avg/min/max aliases that infer typed row fields without raw select strings. Cover alias naming, filesystem/docs examples, generated Ruby, and negative tests for wrong owner/type/raw SQL.
acceptance_criteria: Typed aggregate projection API infers result object shapes; common count/sum/avg/min/max alias examples compile without raw SQL; wrong-owner/wrong-type/raw-string cases fail during Haxe compilation; docs explain generated Ruby and when to use explicit Sql.* escapes instead.
close_reason: Implemented Projection.group for typed selected grouped aggregate result rows. Specs infer Array<{...}> row shapes from grouped field refs and Aggregate count/sum/average/minimum/maximum expressions, lower to Rails group(...).pluck(...), reject wrong-owner/raw-string/non-grouped-field cases, and update docs/tests.

title: Design RailsHx typed query expression layer backed by Arel
description: Design a stable RailsHx Expr/Predicate query layer for SQL function ordering and predicates. Public Haxe API should expose typed Expr<TModel,TValue>, Predicate<TModel>, and narrow builders such as Expr.lower(Todo.f.title), not raw Arel. Document tradeoffs for model ownership, value typing, relation chaining, generated Ruby shape, and interaction with the SQL/string escape policy. Include examples for order, whereExpr, whereNotExpr, future select/group/having, and explicit Sql.* escapes.
notes: Design remains open for the broader expression layer. First implemented slice establishes the public direction: RailsHx Expr/Predicate are typed Haxe APIs, Arel is generated Ruby backend detail, casual strings stay rejected, and future work should add typed builders/aggregates/having before explicit Sql.* escapes. Use the active_record_model smoke and docs/railshx-query-guide.md as the current contract.
close_reason: Completed RailsHx typed query expression design: documented Expr<TModel,TValue>/Predicate<TModel> as the public API, Arel as generated Ruby backend detail, model/value type safety, SQL escape policy through Sql.unsafe*, generated Ruby shape, and constraints for projection/grouping/having follow-up work. Linked from query guide, SQL/string policy, projection/grouping design, and roadmap; sql-string-policy CI now guards the design doc.

title: Add typed ActiveRecord minimum and maximum helpers
description: Add typed single-field minimum() and maximum() helpers for ActiveRecord models and relations. They should accept Field<TModel, TValue>, return Null<TValue>, lower field refs to Rails symbols, and reject fields from the wrong model through Haxe typing.
acceptance_criteria: Relation exposes minimum<TValue>(field:Field<TModel,TValue>):Null<TValue> and maximum<TValue>(field:Field<TModel,TValue>):Null<TValue>; @:railsModel macro generates matching static helpers; compiler lowers Todo.minimum(Todo.f.id), Todo.maximum(Todo.f.title), and relation.maximum(Todo.f.id) to Rails symbol calls; active_record_model smoke asserts generated Ruby and invalid wrong-owner field usage fails; docs/README/compatibility/inventory mention typed min/max; snapshots are updated.
close_reason: Added typed model/relation minimum() and maximum() helpers with Rails symbol lowering, wrong-owner field negative coverage, docs, and snapshots.

title: Add explicit RailsHx Sql escape hatch APIs
description: Define explicit, auditable SQL escape hatches for cases the typed Expr/Predicate builders cannot yet represent. Candidate APIs include Sql.raw/fragment/literal with scary names, strict docs, examples, and negative tests proving casual strings remain rejected on where/order/select/group/having. Prefer typed builders first; raw SQL must be opt-in and easy for audits/LLMs to find.
notes: Implementing explicit RailsHx SQL escape hatch APIs: Sql<TModel,TKind>, SqlWhere/SqlOrder phantom kinds, whereSql/whereNotSql/orderSql/reorderSql relation entrypoints, compiler lowering, smoke tests, docs, and SQL policy coverage.
close_reason: Implemented explicit RailsHx SQL escape hatch APIs: Sql<TModel,TKind> with SqlWhere/SqlOrder phantom kinds, whereSql/whereNotSql/orderSql/reorderSql, compiler lowering to Rails query methods, negative smoke tests for plain strings/wrong owner/wrong kind, docs, inventory, SQL policy gate updates, and refreshed snapshots.

close_reason: Added typed model all() relation entrypoint, ActiveRecord smoke assertions, docs, compatibility/inventory updates, and snapshot coverage.

title: Document RailsHx architecture contract

title: Implement RailsHx typed ActionView template layer

notes: 2026-06-11: Added typed external Rails template interop via Template.external(path):Template<TLocals>. ViewMacro and HHX partial/component lowering now accept Template.external alongside Template.named, preserving typed locals checks while emitting normal Rails render calls and not owning/emitting the external ERB artifact. Added rails_interop_app proving Haxe->legacy ERB, Haxe->legacy Ruby externs, legacy ERB->generated HHX partials, and legacy ERB->generated Haxe service calls. Verified npm run test:rails-interop, REQUIRE_RAILS=1 npm run test:rails-interop under rbenv Ruby 3.3.11, npm run test:todoapp-rails, npm run test:stdlib-inventory, REQUIRE_RAILS=1 npm run test:rails-integration, npm run test:strict-boundaries, ci:version-sync, and ci:release-contracts.

title: Implement RailsHx typed migration DSL and generator

Start with generated extern helpers from Rails-owned config/routes.rb; only add Haxe-owned route emission after helper sync is deterministic and tested.

Parser now handles carried resource prefixes, namespaces, member/collection helpers, nested resource params, multi-verb routes, optional segments, glob params, duplicate helper prefixes, mount-like rows, and internal rails_* skips. description: Turn the current scripts/rails seeds into RailsHx adoption tooling inspired by haxe.elixir.codex's project/scaffold generators and docs. Provide install, compile/watch, model, controller, migration, scaffold, and route-sync commands through npm scripts and/or hxruby rake tasks/gem entrypoints.

Added typed service extern and Template.external wrapper scaffolding, npm/rake entrypoints, gem packaging, docs, and smoke coverage proving generated wrappers compile and Rails-owned ERB is not overwritten. Filed haxe.ruby-wpi.8.2 for Rails::Generators integration and smarter discovery.

Keep the current explicit npm/rake wrappers as stable entrypoints, but make installed Rails apps feel first-class via normal rails generate UX.

Added suggest-only adopt discovery, no-Rails plus optional real-Rails smoke coverage, gem packaging assertions, and docs for Rails-style generator UX. Selected services can pass --service-source; the generator fail-closes on missing files, parses Ruby with Ripper without executing app code, emits constructors, instance methods, and self-methods with Dynamic/scalar-default types, marks ambiguous signatures as TODO, and exposes the feature through CLI, Rails generator, and rake task surfaces.

description: Create a RailsHx integration app/test gate that proves Haxe-authored Rails models, migrations, controllers, params, routes, and generated Ruby run in Rails. Mirror haxe.elixir.codex's end-to-end Phoenix/Ecto examples and sentinel tests, adapted to Rails db:migrate and rails test.

title: Design typed ActionMailer parameterized mailers and previews
description: Design and implement or explicitly defer typed RailsHx support for ActionMailer.with(...), params access, preview classes, and generated preview/test-helper UX. The API should lower to ordinary Rails parameterized mailers and previews while preserving typed Haxe locals/options.
